prospects.name: Fernando Mendoza
prospects.scoutingReport:
Fernando Mendoza, a redshirt junior quarterback, has been making waves in the collegiate football scene with his exceptional performances. With the ideal size of 6’5” and 225 pounds, Mendoza possesses the physical attributes that NFL teams covet in a quarterback. His intelligence on the field is evident in his pre-snap reads, audibles, pocket presence, and ability to excel in two-minute situations. Mendoza also boasts a rare combination of a quick release and a strong arm, allowing him to make ‘NFL throws’ like a hole shot from the far hash. Despite finishing 13-for-23 for 233 yards in a recent game against Iowa, Mendoza consistently showcases his ability to attack all areas of the field with precision.

Transferring from California to Indiana for the 2025 season, Mendoza faced high expectations following Indiana's Cinderella run to the college playoffs. However, he has risen to the challenge, throwing 16 touchdowns to just one interception in the first five games of the season. His confidence as a conductor on the field is palpable, and he remains unflustered even in high-pressure situations. While he may not have the explosiveness of other quarterbacks, Mendoza's steady performance and ability to deliver accurate long strikes down the field make him a formidable force on the field.
One area where Mendoza could improve is in his decision-making, as he occasionally forces throws into tight windows, leading to turnovers. However, his awareness in the pocket and quick, efficient throwing mechanics allow him to escape pressure and deliver accurate passes even on the move. Mendoza's arm strength is another standout trait, enabling him to make all the necessary throws with great velocity and precision. His ability to adjust his arm angles and trajectory to fit passes into tight windows showcases his advanced ball placement skills.
While Mendoza excels in Frank Cignetti’s offense, which emphasizes rhythm throws and quick decisions, questions remain about his ability to cr...

prospects.name: Arvell Reese
prospects.scoutingReport:
One of the most exciting prospects to watch in the 2025 draft class is Arvell Reese, an off-ball linebacker from Ohio State who has been turning heads with his exceptional blend of size, athleticism, and versatility. Reese's impact is evident in every game, whether he's spying the quarterback, blitzing, dropping into coverage, or mirroring the run. At 6'4" and 243 pounds, Reese possesses the physical tools that NFL teams covet in a linebacker, with team sources praising his instincts, size, and athleticism. He has emerged as a game-wrecker for the Buckeyes, showcasing elite speed, range, and playmaking ability on the field.

Reese's versatility to excel in multiple roles within the front seven has increased his draft stock significantly. He has been lauded for his ability to handle everything from covering slot receivers to rushing the passer on third downs, making him an ideal fit for the modern NFL where linebackers are required to be versatile playmakers. While Reese may not fit the traditional mold of a Mike linebacker, his ability to impact the game in a variety of ways has set him apart as a top prospect in the upcoming draft.
One of the key strengths of Reese's game is his prowess as a pass rusher. With 4.5 sacks and 16 pressures this season, he has shown a knack for getting after the quarterback and disrupting opposing offenses. His speed and explosiveness off the edge make him a nightmare for offensive linemen to handle, while his physicality and tackling ability make him a force against the run. Reese's ability to spy mobile quarterbacks and mirror their movements adds another dimension to his game, further enhancing his value as a defensive playmaker.
While Reese has garnered attention for his physical gifts and on-field production, there are areas of his game that still require refinement. His coverage skills have been a point of scrutiny, with a PFF grade of 58.0 in that area this season. However, with his athletic traits and potential for growth, Reese...

prospects.name: David Bailey
prospects.scoutingReport:
David Bailey, a standout pass rusher, showcased his elite skills against Kansas, racking up three sacks and earning the highest pass-rushing grade of the week. His combination of speed, twitch, and natural ability to hunt quarterbacks make him a force to be reckoned with on the field. Despite not being a household name during his time at Stanford, Bailey excelled in a pass-rushing specialist role, displaying an explosive first step that sets him apart from others in his class. His transfer to Texas Tech has allowed him to shine even more, leading the FBS in pressures and proving himself as a dominant force on defense. Bailey's ability to disrupt plays with his quickness and bend around the edge has caught the attention of NFL scouts, who predict he could be a first-round pick.

Known as one of the best pure edge rushers in college football, Bailey's performance this season has been nothing short of exceptional. With an impressive 44 pressures, nine sacks, and 25 quarterback hurries, he has shown his prowess as a pass rusher. While Bailey may lack some run defense skills, his pass-rushing abilities make him a valuable asset on third downs. Standing at 6'3" and 250 pounds, Bailey's twitched-up athleticism and high motor make him a perfect fit for a 3-4 system. His compact build and explosive speed allow him to consistently win off the line and chase down plays with relentless effort. Although Bailey's frame may limit him to a rotational role in the NFL, his competitiveness and drive give him a strong chance to make an impact as a late-day 2 or early day 3 steal.
Bailey's arsenal of pass-rushing moves, including club-swims, swipe-rips, spins, and pure speed rushes, make him a nightmare for offensive linemen to defend against. His explosive get-off and ability to bend around the corner set him apart from other edge rushers in the 2026 class. Bailey's first step is unmatched, allowing him to gain ground quickly and disrupt plays in the backfield. His quick wins and loose...

prospects.name: Jeremiyah Love
prospects.scoutingReport:
Jeremiyah Love, a standout running back for Notre Dame, has taken the collegiate football world by storm with his explosive playmaking abilities. Standing at 6'0" and 214 pounds, Love boasts a rare combination of speed, agility, and power that make him a nightmare for opposing defenses. Love has proven himself to be a focal point of the offense, with the ability to carry the team on his back and create explosive plays out of seemingly routine situations. His track-verified speed and natural instincts on the field set him apart as a dynamic offensive threat.

Love's efficiency as a runner is evident in his ability to read the defense, find openings, and exploit them with his burst and acceleration. His one-cut style allows him to quickly get upfield and challenge defenders with his breakaway speed. Love excels in zone and outside zone schemes, where he can stretch the defense horizontally and create opportunities for big gains. His vision, patience, and elusiveness make him a potent weapon in the ground game, while his versatility as a receiver adds another dimension to his skill set.
While Love's pass-catching ability is solid, there is room for improvement in his route running and expanding his role beyond screens and check-downs. Additionally, his pass protection skills need refinement to become a more complete three-down back at the next level. Despite these areas of improvement, Love's athleticism and playmaking potential make him a top prospect in the 2026 NFL Draft class.
Love's physicality, contact balance, and finishing ability set him apart as a back who can thrive in various offensive schemes. He has shown the willingness to absorb hits, break tackles, and fight for extra yards, showcasing his competitive toughness and drive to succeed. Love's speed and agility allow him to outmaneuver defenders and turn routine plays into big gains, making him a constant threat to score on any given play.
Looking ahead to the NFL, Love's skill set translates well to the m...

prospects.name: Francis Mauigoa
prospects.scoutingReport:
Francis Mauigoa is a standout offensive line prospect with the physical attributes and skill set to excel at the next level. At 6-foot-6 and 315 pounds, Mauigoa boasts a big, thick body that is ideal for playing in the NFL. His arm length is adequate for offensive tackle play, and he possesses a wide base and footwork that allow him to neutralize bull rushes effectively. Mauigoa is not afraid to dictate contact with a strong long arm, keeping his weight on his inside foot to be ready for inside counters. However, he can sometimes be too heavily on his toes, leading to lunges and leg drives. Improving his anticipation of rushers' movements and locating help on the field will be key to his development.

Mauigoa's experience and development trajectory suggest that he is still unlocking his full potential as a player. His motor, size, and skills make him a promising offensive tackle prospect for a man- or gap-blocking scheme. His immediate impact is likely to be felt most significantly in the run game, where his ability to move defenders and create vertical displacement can transform an offense's ground attack. While still refining his pass protection techniques, Mauigoa has shown the movement skills and anchor strength to handle edge rushers in the NFL. His athleticism and power profile indicate that he could develop into a perennial Pro Bowl-caliber tackle.
With his diverse array of blocking techniques and experience in Miami's offensive system, Mauigoa is well-prepared for the transition to the next level. His combination of athleticism, power, and footwork make him an asset in both pass protection and run schemes. Teams picking early in the first round, such as Jacksonville, Carolina, or Tennessee, should view Mauigoa as a cornerstone piece around which to build their offensive line. Mauigoa's ceiling as a guard is also worth considering, given his thick frame, lower-body strength, and movement skills that could make him a Pro Bowl-level player from day one.
Mauigoa...

prospects.name: Sonny Styles
prospects.scoutingReport:
Sonny Styles is a linebacker for the National Championship Buckeyes who has made significant strides in his transition from safety to linebacker, showcasing impressive tools and skills that make him a standout player. Standing at 6'4" and weighing 243 pounds, Styles has proven himself as a formidable force on the field, moving with the agility and speed of a safety while delivering the physicality of a linebacker. His ability to cover ground, close in on ball carriers, and track the ball in both run defense and pass coverage has been instrumental in his success as a linebacker, and his fluid movement skills have helped him excel in various defensive roles.

Styles' versatility on the field has been a key factor in his development as a linebacker, with his ability to handle multiple responsibilities making him a valuable asset to any defense. His size-speed combination and positional flexibility have allowed him to impact the run game effectively, showcasing natural leverage and pursuit speed that translate well to the professional level. While he has shown significant growth in coverage, particularly in zone coverage, there is still room for improvement, especially in man coverage against quicker targets.
One of Styles' standout strengths is his athleticism, which allows him to cover tight ends and running backs in man coverage with ease, as well as carry wide receivers on vertical routes when playing Tampa 2. His speed and agility enable him to close in on ball carriers quickly and make open-field tackles with precision. Styles' physicality against the run, coupled with his ability to take on blocks and slip past offensive linemen, has made him a formidable presence in the box.
While Styles has shown significant potential as a linebacker, there are areas where he can continue to grow and refine his game. His tackling consistency, coverage skills, and ability to stack and shed blocks are aspects that will require ongoing development as he transitions to the NFL. How...

prospects.name: Rueben Bain
prospects.scoutingReport:
Rueben Bain Jr. is a defensive force that evaluators dream of discovering, transitioning from an injury casualty to a potential crown jewel of the draft. His dominance on film showcases a defender operating on a level above his college competition, decimating protection schemes and overwhelming offensive linemen with his hands. Bain's ability to process protection concepts and adjust his attack angles like a seasoned veteran sets him apart as a formidable force on the field. His versatility allows for maximum impact when deployed as a moveable weapon rather than a stationary edge, showcasing elite production regardless of alignment. With the inside rush package he's developed, Bain turns third-and-long situations into nightmares for interior linemen, showcasing a rare combination of quickness and power.

Florida State's head coach has publicly hailed Bain as one of the nation's elite defenders, a sentiment echoed by opposing coaches who campaign for his draft status, a testament to the fear he instills in opponents. Despite concerns regarding his weight and potential position fit in the NFL, Bain's blend of size, power, and athleticism makes him a standout prospect. His ability to excel as a run defender and pass rusher alike, with a variety of moves in his arsenal, positions him as a top-tier player with All-Pro potential. Bain's physical dominance, relentless motor, and versatility make him a highly sought-after prospect with the potential to anchor a franchise's pass rush for years to come.
Through his explosive play on the field, Bain has quickly risen to prominence as one of the top defensive prospects in college football. His versatility along the defensive front, coupled with his ability to align in various stances, showcases his dominance as a run defender. Bain's quickness and power allow him to overwhelm offensive linemen and disrupt plays in the backfield, making him a force to be reckoned with. As a pass rusher, Bain's alignment versatility and array of ...

prospects.name: Carnell Tate
prospects.scoutingReport:
Carnell Tate, a standout junior wide receiver at The Ohio State University, hailing from Chicago, Illinois, has been making waves in the college football scene with his exceptional skills and football acumen. Known for his precise route running and high football IQ, Tate's prowess as a pass-catcher is unmatched. He possesses an incredible catch radius, allowing him to secure every target thrown his way with precision. While Tate has some areas for improvement, such as adding more weight to his frame for increased strength, his strengths far outweigh any weaknesses. He thrives in a system that values technique and nuance over pure athleticism, making him a valuable asset to any team.

As the WR2 for the Ohio State Buckeyes, Tate has shown that being the secondary option can actually work in his favor. With talented receivers like Jeremiah Smith and Emeka Egbuka drawing attention, Tate often benefits from favorable coverages, allowing him to exploit matchups and make explosive plays. In 2024, despite playing behind Smith and Egbuka, Tate showcased his abilities by recording impressive stats, indicating his potential for significant production in the upcoming season.
Standing at 6'3", Tate utilizes his height and catch radius to excel at high-pointing balls and securing contested catches. His game speed surpasses his 40-time, and he is renowned for being one of the best route runners in his class. Tate's versatility allows him to play both inside and outside, making him a valuable asset for any NFL offense. While he may not be a deep threat, Tate's reliability, versatility, and professional attitude make him a desirable addition to any receiving corps.
In NFL schemes that prioritize route precision and intermediate excellence, Tate shines as a high-floor possession receiver. His efficiency in route running and understanding of professional concepts make him a natural fit for systems that require receivers to hit landmarks precisely and outsmart defenders with nuanced r...

prospects.name: Caleb Downs
prospects.scoutingReport:
Caleb Downs is a generational talent at the safety position, drawing comparisons to greats like Eric Berry and Budda Baker. At 6’0” and 205 pounds, Downs possesses elite instincts and positional versatility that make him a game-changer for any defense. His football intelligence and rare athletic gifts set him apart, allowing him to align anywhere on the field from deep safety to slot corner to linebacker depth. He reads route combinations like a quarterback, takes flawless pursuit angles, and triggers downhill without hesitation. His versatility makes him a perfect fit in modern NFL schemes that require adaptive solutions to matchup problems. Downs thrives in disguise-heavy defenses that value interchangeable safeties, showcasing exceptional coverage ability and physicality against the run.

The Ohio State standout is more than just a safety; he’s a defensive weapon that transcends traditional positional limitations. Downs' exceptional tackling ability, combined with his range and closing speed in coverage, make him a dynamic playmaker who can shut down the deep part of the field and cover a ton of ground. Whether it's defending perimeter runs, disrupting passing plays, or returning kicks, Downs excels in every aspect of the game. His ability to read and react quickly, along with his elite ball location and confidence in his reads, make him a rare talent in the secondary.
Downs' impact goes beyond his on-field performance; he's a natural leader with elite football IQ and a strong work ethic. Coming from a football family with a brother in the NFL and a father who played in the league, Downs is a film junkie and locker-room tone-setter. His versatility, intelligence, and physicality make him a safe prospect with a high floor and a ceiling that reaches gold jacket territory. The team that secures Downs' services will be getting more than just a safety; they'll be acquiring a culture-changing presence who elevates the play of those around him. Downs projects as an imme...

prospects.name: Spencer Fano
prospects.scoutingReport:
Utah tackle Spencer Fano is a standout player with incredible athleticism and potential. Standing at 6’6” and weighing 308 pounds, Fano is a lean and quick athlete with exceptional footwork and agility. His ability to hold his own in both pass protection and run blocking makes him a highly sought-after prospect. While he excels in protection due to his quickness off the line and adeptness at picking up late blitzers, Fano could benefit from improving his strength and power to become a more explosive blocker. His run blocking is solid, particularly in direct runs, but his athletic profile suggests he could thrive in zone schemes as well. Fano has a knack for playing with leverage and grit at the point of attack, allowing him to excel at the second level. However, he needs to work on consistency and dominance in executing rushing schemes.

As a true freshman, Fano earned the starting left tackle job for the Utes before transitioning to the right side, where he has truly shined. His reputation as a run-game savant is well-deserved, as he demonstrates exceptional technique, leverage, and footwork to create angles and move defenders off the ball. Fano's pass protection skills are reliable, though he could benefit from improving his hand usage and assertiveness at the point of attack. While some question his ability to play left tackle in the NFL due to arm length concerns, Fano's versatility and technical prowess suggest he could excel at either tackle spot or transition to guard if needed.
Fano's elite athleticism and power make him a scheme-versatile prospect with Pro Bowl upside. While he may need to add mass to anchor against pro-level power rushers, his combination of intelligence, athleticism, and aggressive blocking style set him apart from other prospects. Additionally, Fano's exceptional run blocking and pass protection skills, paired with his ability to thrive in various schemes, make him a Day 1 starter with the potential for immediate impact. Despite some are...

prospects.name: Mansoor Delane
prospects.scoutingReport:
Mansoor Delane, a cornerback for the LSU Tigers, has garnered attention for his standout performances in the SEC after transferring from Virginia Tech. Known for his sticky coverage in man defense, Delane brings physicality and intelligence to the position, allowing him to force incompletions, break up passes, and make interceptions. Despite his average speed and shorter arms, Delane's coverage skills and competitive temperament make him a challenging target for opposing offenses. His success against Clemson showcased his ability to shut down top receivers, prevent separation, and make plays on the football at various levels of the field.

Delane's football IQ, tackling ability, and assignment soundness are notable strengths, along with his deceleration and ball tracking skills. However, concerns about his frame, high-cut transitions, and struggles with press coverage consistency have been highlighted. Delane's physical tools, including size, strength, and speed, make him a promising NFL prospect with the potential to be a reliable starter. While his ball skills and competitive nature are assets, he will need to work on minimizing penalties and improving his press coverage technique to excel at the next level.
Delane's versatility, fluid athleticism, and competitive mindset have positioned him as a top cornerback prospect in the upcoming draft. He excels in both man and zone coverages, using his quickness, lateral agility, and instincts to mirror receivers and make plays on the ball. Despite occasional lapses in coverage and struggles with larger receivers, Delane's potential as an impact player in multiple defensive schemes is evident. His adaptability, competitive nature, and physical attributes make him a valuable asset for teams in need of a reliable defensive back who can contribute immediately.
Overall, Delane's combination of instincts, athleticism, and competitive temperament make him a prime candidate for an early-round selection in the draft. While he may ...

prospects.name: Makai Lemon
prospects.scoutingReport:
Makai Lemon, the junior receiver for USC, has caught the attention of NFL scouts and analysts alike with his impressive performances on the field. His transition to the professional level is expected to be smoother than most receiver prospects due to his technical foundation and proven ability to consistently separate from defenders. Lemon's mastery of the slot position, combined with his developing outside skills, provides him with multiple pathways to early playing time in offensive systems that value versatility and reliable hands. His success against both man and zone coverage in college indicates his readiness for the more complex defensive schemes of the NFL.

Teams that utilize spread concepts and quick-game packages will find Lemon's skill set particularly appealing as it translates well to professional passing games where decision-making speed is crucial. Lemon's special teams contributions add value to his roster spot while he continues to develop into a full-time offensive weapon. His college production and measurable athleticism create a solid foundation for professional success, positioning him well for early selection and sustained NFL performance.
Standing at 5-foot-11 and 195 pounds, Lemon's alignment versatility, quickness, and detailed route-running abilities make him a slot-receiver prospect due to his ability to align both on the outside and in the slot. Lemon excels against zone defense, showcasing high football IQ and understanding of how to attack defensive backs while reading coverage effectively. His precision in route running, ability to manipulate defenders' leverage, and sharp deceleration at the stem's peak create natural separation against tight coverage at short and intermediate levels, making him a reliable target in various offensive schemes.
While Lemon may lack the top-end speed to consistently separate vertically, his agility, acceleration, and shiftiness after the catch make him a valuable playmaker in space. His reliable hands, ...

prospects.name: Jordyn Tyson
prospects.scoutingReport:
Jordyn Tyson, a redshirt junior wide receiver prospect for the 2026 NFL Draft, boasts legitimate WR1 potential as he enters the 2025 college football season. Beginning his collegiate career at Colorado before transferring to Arizona State, Tyson showcased his talent with a breakout 2024 season, amassing 75 catches, 1,101 yards, and 10 touchdowns. His prototypical frame, athleticism, and natural instincts make him a high-end playmaker for the Sun Devils, excelling in both slot and boundary alignments. Tyson's versatility allows him to line up at various positions, utilizing smooth and efficient releases to create separation from defenders.

His route running is a standout feature, with impeccable timing, body control, and the ability to win against press-man coverage. Tyson's basketball background shines through in his footwork, spatial awareness, and contested catch prowess. He consistently high-points the ball, showcasing strong hands and the agility to adjust in the air. Despite concerns about winning against press coverage, Tyson's detailed route running and competitive toughness set him apart.
Tyson's explosive playmaking ability, YAC potential, and contested catch skills make him a dangerous threat in any offense. His quick acceleration, vision, and elusiveness after the catch highlight his versatility and impact in the passing game. While Tyson's size may not be imposing, his ability to outfight defenders for contested catches and his balanced skill set draw comparisons to Amon-Ra St. Brown.
In 2025, Tyson aims to address areas of improvement, such as reducing drops, enhancing separation ability, and demonstrating consistent football IQ. His quickness, agility, route nuance, and competitive toughness make him a dynamic receiver with potential for a top-ten selection in the 2026 NFL Draft. With a refined physicality against press coverage and continued development, Tyson could solidify his status as a first-round talent.
Despite past injuries, Tyson's explosive...

prospects.name: Jermod McCoy
prospects.scoutingReport:
Jermod McCoy, a standout cornerback from Tennessee, has been making waves in the football world despite a setback due to an ACL tear in January that has prevented him from playing in the 2025 season. At 6'0" and weighing 193 pounds, McCoy's athleticism, fluid movements, and ability to match receivers' actions in coverage make him a top prospect for the upcoming NFL Draft. His impressive tape from 2024 showcases his skills in man coverage and zone defense, where he has excelled in creating turnovers and disrupting passing lanes.

McCoy's versatility in different defensive schemes, particularly those emphasizing zone patterns, highlights his football IQ and spatial awareness, allowing him to process information quickly and anticipate plays. Despite concerns about his recovery from the ACL injury and questions regarding his deep speed, McCoy's instincts, ball skills, and ability to read quarterbacks make him a valuable asset for any team looking for a shutdown cornerback at the next level. His knack for creating turnovers and his high football IQ set him apart from other prospects in the draft.
Known for his physicality in press coverage and his ability to match up against bigger, faster receivers, McCoy has the potential to be a game-changer in the NFL. His explosive athleticism, competitive toughness, and instincts on the field have earned him recognition as a top cornerback prospect, with many projecting him to be the first cornerback off the board in the upcoming draft. While his ACL injury and playing status for the 2025 season remain uncertain, McCoy's talent, size, and playmaking ability suggest that he has what it takes to succeed at the next level and become a legitimate NFL starter.
Despite the concerns surrounding his injury and playing status, McCoy's impressive skill set, including his size, speed, athleticism, and coverage abilities, make him a sought-after prospect in the upcoming NFL Draft. With his fluid movements, competitive nature, and physicality a...

prospects.name: Kenyon Sadiq
prospects.scoutingReport:
Kenyon Sadiq is a talented tight end prospect from the Oregon Ducks, known for his deep-ball production and vertical threat capability that create matchup problems at the professional level. However, there are concerns regarding his blocking inconsistencies and 245-pound frame, which may limit his every-down usage in the NFL. While his route precision suggests immediate contributions as a receiving weapon, tight ends who struggle in protection often get pigeonholed into specific packages rather than featured roles. Sadiq's speed advantage over linebackers becomes more pronounced in the NFL, although his frame limitations may pose challenges against pass rushers who test his anchoring ability. Nonetheless, his competitive spirit and football intelligence provide hope for technical improvements in blocking. The league's shift towards athletic tight ends benefits Sadiq's cause, but baseline competency across all phases remains crucial.

As a pass catcher, Sadiq's versatility is a key asset, lining up everywhere from the slot to out wide, showcasing a reliable, big-bodied target for quarterbacks. His athleticism allows him to stretch the field vertically and make contested catches. Sadiq's ability after the catch makes him elusive in space, showcasing impressive vision and agility to create yards. While he excels as a receiver, there are areas for improvement, such as route precision and focus drops. Additionally, his physicality and finishing attitude in blocking contribute significantly to the run game, dominating defenders with strength, balance, and a relentless approach.
Sadiq's athleticism and versatility are evident in his alignment across various positions, making him a challenging mismatch for defenses. His explosive speed and quickness stress coverages vertically, while his elusiveness and physicality make him a dangerous playmaker in space. Despite his undersized frame, Sadiq's athleticism allows him to compete with larger defenders and excel in different off...

prospects.name: Olaivavega Ioane
prospects.scoutingReport:
Playing with a level of consistency that his teammates on the Penn State offensive line have struggled to match this season, Olaivavega Ioane, who also goes by “Vega,” has stood out as a rock-solid presence in both run blocking and pass protection. A fourth-year junior with a third-round grade that has only risen as the season has progressed, Ioane displayed his prowess in pass protection against Oregon and showcased his ability to dominate in the run game, particularly near the goal line. With a potential to be the first guard drafted, Ioane has established himself as a standout performer for the Nittany Lions.

A second-team All-Big Ten selection in 2024, Ioane is widely regarded as the best pure guard in the draft, boasting an impressive physical stature at 6'4" and 328 pounds. Known for his physicality and mauling style of play, Ioane excels in opening holes in the run game and has been a stalwart pass protector, not allowing a single sack or quarterback hit in over 175 pass-blocking snaps this season. His combination of strength, mass, and mobility make him a formidable force on the offensive line, with the potential to be a first-round pick in the upcoming NFL Draft.
Ioane's versatility is another key aspect of his game, having taken snaps at both guard positions, as well as tackle and center. His NFL-caliber frame and refined build, which includes shedding unnecessary weight heading into the 2025 season, allow him to excel in a variety of roles along the offensive line. While he possesses a strong anchor and powerful base, Ioane must work on his hand usage and strike timing to improve his overall effectiveness in both pass protection and run blocking. Despite some issues with leverage and agility, Ioane's high football IQ and understanding of blocking schemes make him a valuable asset for any team.
With the potential to be a quality starter in the NFL, Ioane's technical refinement will be crucial in unlocking his full potential. His ability to stonewall elite...

prospects.name: Keldric Faulk
prospects.scoutingReport:
Keldric Faulk, a true junior prospect from Auburn, has established himself as one of the top defensive linemen in college football. Since his freshman year, Faulk's impact has steadily increased, showcasing a blend of length, quickness, and a violent temperament that make him a potential high-end defensive end at the next level. Faulk's versatility is evident in his ability to excel from various alignments along the defensive front, whether as an interior disruptor or an edge rusher. His prowess against the run is a standout trait, setting a hard edge, shedding blocks, and displaying high effort in pursuit. As a pass rusher, Faulk's speed-to-power conversion, length, quickness, and power moves make him a disruptive force, although there are questions about his elite athleticism around the edge in the NFL.

Faulk's skill set extends beyond the field, with his size, power, and movement skills making him a coveted prospect for teams valuing versatility in their defensive line. His three-down impact, combined with his high motor, suffocating tackling ability, and malicious hand strikes, further enhance his value as a cornerstone defender. Faulk's pass-rushing arsenal, while still developing, shows promise, with a focus on refining his counter moves and maximizing his natural gifts. His potential as a five-technique end in a 3-4 defense or a strongside end in a 4-3 scheme positions him as a valuable asset for any defense.
Despite his impressive attributes, Faulk continues to refine his game, seeking out pass rush specialists and working with pro rushers to elevate his skills. His dedication to improvement, along with his on-field performance, has garnered attention from NFL evaluators, who recognize his potential as a game-changing defensive presence. Faulk's blend of size, strength, speed, and versatility, coupled with his strong work ethic and commitment to excellence, sets him apart as a prospect with a high floor and All-Pro upside. As he continues to hone his craft ...

prospects.name: Akheem Mesidor
prospects.scoutingReport:
Miami's defensive line boasts a standout player in Rueben Bain Jr., projected as a first-round pick, but it was Mesidor who stole the show in the recent win over Notre Dame. His performance included 1.5 sacks on the final two plays from scrimmage, showcasing his pass-rush prowess with a 37.5% win rate, ranking fourth in the FBS. Standing at 6'3" and weighing 280 pounds, Mesidor's agility and athleticism were on full display, earning him recognition as a player to watch. Despite being a sixth-year senior, Mesidor may not have the most impressive resume, but his performance against Notre Dame certainly caught the attention of many.

Looking ahead to the NFL, Mesidor is seen as a versatile defensive lineman capable of contributing in various schemes. His pass-rushing abilities from multiple positions make him a valuable asset to coordinators seeking to create mismatches. While his size may have limitations, his quickness and relentless pursuit suggest that he can make an impact early on as a rotational player. Teams with aggressive, one-gap defensive schemes will likely find Mesidor particularly appealing due to his first-step quickness and disruptive nature.
However, Mesidor will need to prove his ability to handle the run consistently to become an every-down player in the league. His draft stock may also be influenced by his medical reports following a shortened 2023 season. If he can demonstrate his full health and retain his explosiveness, Mesidor could potentially be a day two selection with the potential to exceed expectations. Teams searching for a dynamic player to bolster their defensive line rotation may find Mesidor's combination of production, versatility, and relentless effort enticing. His pass-rush arsenal, quickness, and pursuit skills make him a force to be reckoned with, showcasing his ability to disrupt plays both in the passing and running game. However, despite his impressive physical attributes and versatile skill set, Hill has not quite replicated his dominant 2024 form this season. Standing at 6'3" and weighing 238 pounds, Hill shares many similarities with Reese, showcasing a combination of size, athleticism, and the ability to contribute in various defensive roles. He is known for his downhill style of play, impeccable instincts, and controlled violence on the field. A standout feature of Hill's game is his exceptional speed, with a reported low-4.4s time in the 40-yard dash. This speed allows him to be a sideline-to-sideline force in run defense and a disruptive presence on blitzes. As he continues to refine his game and grow his rush nuance, Thomas has the potential to be a difference-maker at the next level. With a high-cut frame that makes change of direction tougher, Bernard excels at creating separation with minimal wasted movement, showcasing soft hands, natural ball skills, and a physicality that enhances the running game. Despite his journey through different college programs, Bernard has solidified his status as a promising draft prospect. Known for his nasty playing style, Dunker thrives on imposing his will on defenders, particularly in the run game. Dunker's NFL potential hinges on positional clarity and technical refinement. prospects.name: Domonique Orange
prospects.scoutingReport:
Dominique Orange, affectionately nicknamed "Big Citrus," has been a standout player for Iowa State, showcasing his skills as both a pass rusher and run defender. Standing at an impressive 6'4" and weighing 325 pounds, Orange possesses explosive athleticism, as evidenced by his 34-inch vertical jump. His size and strength make him a force to be reckoned with on the field, dictating the line of scrimmage and disrupting opposing offenses.

While Orange may not be the flashiest pass rusher, having recorded only one sack in his career and limited pressures this season, his ability to displace blockers with a powerful punch and use a variety of moves like the swim, club, and rip techniques showcases his potential. Orange's true strength lies in his run defense, where he excels at disrupting zone runs and generating tackles for loss. While he may not be a high-ceiling prospect, Orange's high floor and dependability make him a valuable asset for any defensive line room, projecting as a late day 2 or early day 3 pick in the NFL Draft.
Overall, Orange's versatility, power, and motor set him apart as one of the most underrated players in the Big 12 conference. As he continues to refine his pass-rushing skills and showcase his ability to stay on the field for more passing downs, Orange's stock as a potential Day 2 selection in the NFL Draft continues to rise. With his combination of size, strength, and football intelligence, Orange has the potential to make a significant impact at the next level as a key rotational piece in a defensive line rotation.

As a Day 3 prospect, Sarratt's average athletic traits may limit his draft stock, but his reputation as a reliable possession receiver who excels in 50-50 ball situations makes him a desirable addition to any team's receiving corps. Standing at 6-foot-2 and 209 pounds, Sarratt's size and build position him as a boundary receiver in the NFL, pending his testing numbers like the 40-yard dash. Despite mixed feedback from scouts, Sarratt's strong hands, route running, and play speed have garnered attention from potential fans, potentially leading to his selection as early as Day 2. Despite some inconsistencies in the run game, Tiernan's efficient angles and controlled play have propelled him into the top-100 mix and continue to elevate his draft stock as he climbs up the rankings.

On the positive side, Igbinosun has shown significant growth in his game, reducing his penalty count and demonstrating a better understanding of the physicality required at the cornerback position. With his excellent instincts, ball skills, and football IQ, Igbinosun has the potential to be a valuable asset for any NFL team. prospects.name: Darrell Jackson Jr.
prospects.scoutingReport:
Darrell Jackson Jr. is a massive and physically imposing defensive tackle at 6’5” and 337 pounds. His size alone makes him a force to be reckoned with in caving pockets and disrupting passing lanes. Jackson excels at using his strength to hold the point against single or double-team blocks, making him a gap-occupying tackle who does the dirty work on the inside. His bull rush is incredibly powerful and when he maintains the correct pad level, he can easily forklift interior linemen off their spot. However, Jackson's technique is still raw, relying more on size and strength rather than refined skill. This limits his potential as an every-down player early on, but his sheer physical tools make him an intriguing developmental prospect with legitimate rotational potential.

Despite his impressive strength and ability to clog running lanes, Jackson will need to work on maintaining a high level of intensity on every snap to reach his full potential. He is not a flashy playmaker but rather a reliable interior starter who can force protection slides and create chaos in opposing pockets. Jackson's game is best when he lands first and keeps the fight on the offense’s half, utilizing a quick counter off the bull rush to keep opponents off balance. With his size, power, and ability to soak up blocks, Jackson has the potential to be a disruptive force in the trenches and make life difficult for opposing offenses all afternoon. In summary, Jackson is a talented prospect with massive frame potential and the tools to become a key contributor on the defensive line with the right development and coaching.

Standing at 5'9" and 229 pounds, Coleman is known for his power, balance, and ability to absorb punishment while still maintaining his speed and agility. Coleman's strengths lie in his contact balance, physical running style, and ability to power through defenders with brute force. While he may lack the top-end speed of other running backs, his quick acceleration, lateral agility, and ability to string cuts together make him a threat in zone-based schemes. His impressive contact balance allows him to break tackles and keep his legs churning, making him a viable option in short-yardage situations. In the open field, Coleman can finish runs with a physical nature, often overpowering defenders in his path.
Despite his strengths as a runner, Coleman's receiving ability is an area of concern. While he can catch passes out of the backfield and is solid in pass protection, his route running and separation from defenders may limit his impact in the passing game. His athleticism and speed also come into question, as he may struggle to outrun defenders or create explosive plays downfield. However, Coleman's work ethic and dedication to improving his game suggest that he has the potential to be a reliable back in the NFL.
In summary, Jonah Coleman's combination of power, balance, vision, and physicality make him a versatile and effective runner who can contribute in multiple rushing schemes. prospects.name: Malik Muhammad

Coordinators who value secondary versatility will appreciate his proficiency in executing both man and zone techniques with equal effectiveness. Although he lacks elite length, Muhammad compensates with outstanding footwork and transitional quickness, enabling him to mirror even the most elusive route runners. His technical prowess in press technique and zone spacing underscores his attention to detail and thorough coaching.

Drawing comparisons to a young Marcus Peters, Muhammad stands out in a cornerback class filled with specialists as a complete defender capable of aligning anywhere in the secondary. His ability to transform a unit's identity and elevate the defensive structure makes him a cornerstone talent with immense potential. With a mix of good awareness, adept hand usage to break up passes, and physicality in coverage, Muhammad is set to become a playmaker for the Texas secondary in 2025. His versatility allows him to effectively contribute to run support and coverage, showcasing his well-rounded skill set. While he thrives in man coverage, Muhammad also displays competence in zone coverage, making him a valuable asset for any defensive coordinator.

When observing Trigg's gameplay between the numbers, it is evident that he poses a significant threat to defensive coordinators, utilizing his deceptive twitch and route-running prowess to outmaneuver opponents. His knack for setting up linebackers with head fakes and explosive plays down the field demonstrates his value as an offensive weapon. Despite questions surrounding his blocking abilities and commitment to the game, Trigg's physical tools suggest that he has the potential to evolve into a versatile tight end capable of creating matchup challenges for opposing defenses.

prospects.name: Deontae Lawson
prospects.scoutingReport:
Lawson, the conductor of Alabama's defense, is a key player in the heart of the team's middle. With excellent pre-snap awareness and strong hands on contact, Lawson is able to swiftly react to plays and make impactful tackles. His ability to play with square positioning, maintain leverage, and effectively navigate through traffic sets the tone for the entire front seven. However, a closer examination of his game reveals some areas for improvement, particularly in coverage where he is more consistent than dominant. While he excels in zone coverage, Lawson struggles at times against tight ends and running backs, and can be overpowered by larger linemen when his pads rise. By refining his technique and improving his performance on third downs, Lawson has the potential to become a reliable three-down player who elevates the play of those around him.

Despite facing some challenges early in his career, Lawson has steadily improved his game and established himself as a valuable asset on the defense. As a green-dot signal caller, he has been instrumental in orchestrating the team's defensive efforts, and his PFF run-defense grades have shown consistent growth over the years. Lawson's ability to quickly change direction, coupled with his tenacity and competitiveness against larger blockers, makes him a formidable presence on the field. Additionally, his proficiency in blitzing and covering players out of the backfield highlights his versatility as a linebacker.
While Lawson's weight profile falls below the NFL average, he compensates for this with his explosive speed and agility, particularly as a blitzer. Despite some concerns about his size and durability, Lawson has shown significant progress in his playmaking abilities and run defense, especially during the 2024 season before his injury. prospects.name: Will Lee III
prospects.scoutingReport:
Texas A&M's defensive showdown with Auburn may not have been the high-scoring affair many were expecting, but it did provide an opportunity for cornerback Will Lee III to showcase his skills against top-tier competition. Going up against talented wide receivers like Cam Coleman and Eric Singleton Jr. was a crucial test for Lee's NFL potential, and he rose to the occasion. In both man and zone coverage, Lee demonstrated his ability to stick with receivers, using his length and play strength to limit separation and disrupt routes. While he did give up a big play to Singleton, overall, his coverage was solid and consistent throughout the game. 

This performance against Auburn adds to Lee's resume as one of the top cornerback prospects in the 2026 class. Despite some inconsistencies earlier in the season, Lee has shown flashes of brilliance that have NFL teams excited about his future. One play that stands out is his impressive coverage against Notre Dame, where he displayed patience and balance in defending a crucial third-and-goal situation, ultimately forcing the Irish to settle for a field goal. While Lee may not possess elite speed or fluidity, his height, length, and natural cover instincts make him a valuable asset for any NFL team looking to bolster their secondary.
As the season progresses, Lee will have more opportunities to prove himself against tough competition and solidify his status as one of the top cornerback prospects in the upcoming draft. If he can maintain the level of play he showed against Auburn and continue to refine his skills, there's no doubt that he will be a sought-after prospect come draft day. Will Lee III's combination of physical tools and football IQ make him a seasoned cover man with the potential to make a significant impact at the next level.

The performance against Oregon raised questions about Allar’s decision-making and effectiveness in high-pressure situations. Allar's struggles against top opponents, combined with his limited development and inconsistencies, have led to doubts about his draft stock and potential as a franchise quarterback.
Critics have pointed to Allar’s accuracy, footwork, and decision-making as areas in need of improvement. While he possesses an elite frame and arm talent, his mechanics and mobility have been identified as weaknesses that could hinder his success at the next level. prospects.name: Austin Barber
prospects.scoutingReport: Barber is a well-built offensive tackle prospect who is a smooth mover in all facets. He fits best in a zone-blocking scheme. His low, wide pre-snap stance shows off his natural flexibility, and his technique gives him consistent pad-level advantages. He shows effective technique as a run blocker with hand placement and leg drive, but he could stand to get stronger in that and other areas. On a similar note, he must be able to maintain his blocks longer. Barber plays like a bouncer guarding the club door: violent, aggressive, and utterly unrelenting. His hands are weapons, striking defenders with precision and power. The Gators’ left tackle has developed into one of the most consistent SEC linemen, and his strength base is tremendous. He’s not quite as twitchy as Lomu, but he brings NFL-ready anchor strength and rarely loses leverage battles. Barber projects as a Day 2 starter with immediate swing tackle upside and long-term left tackle potential in gap-heavy offenses. Barber has terrific length, size, and athleticism. If Florida's offense is to remain efficient and on schedule, it'll need offensive linemen like Barber to win up front. Barber endured difficult outings against Miami and Texas A&M, but his traits and experience—he’s started over 30 games in the past four seasons—keep him in the top 50. The 6' 6", 314-pound Barber has smooth feet, consistent pad level, and the strength necessary to anchor in pass protection. He uses his body well as a run blocker, be it sealing lanes or taking advantageous angles, and he can kick out edge rushers to open lanes. Barber has struggled with speed at times this season, a surprising revelation given his foot quickness and athleticism in space. How much weight evaluators place on his isolated poor performances will determine how high he goes. prospects.name: Isaiah World
prospects.scoutingReport:
Isaiah World is a rare anomaly as the number one transfer portal player hailing from Nevada, now taking his talents to Eugene, Oregon in pursuit of further development. With the size and movement skills desired in an NFL offensive tackle prospect, World's physical style promises to shine against stiffer Big Ten competition come 2025. His aggressive and assertive tackle play, long arms to match his towering frame, and good hand-fighting skills paint a promising picture, although his 29 penalties from 2022-2024 highlight areas for improvement. Despite being a late bloomer, World's emergence and potential as an intangibly rich offensive line prospect have drawn attention, especially in pass protection. While still refining his game, World's agility, kick step, and foot speed give him a high ceiling in pass protection, with room for growth in run blocking.

World's blend of athleticism, power, and ideal size make him an enticing prospect for NFL teams seeking a developmental tackle who could evolve into a quality starter with proper coaching. His pass-blocking prowess and improving lateral movement suggest a specialized role early in his career, particularly in pass-first schemes. While his basketball background contributes to his rare coordination for his size, World's ongoing development in power and leverage techniques remains a focus. His fifth-year growth at Oregon against higher-caliber competition hints at a positive trajectory, with potential to excel on the right side where his pass protection skills could shine. With three years of starting experience and a refined awareness, World's explosive athleticism and pass protection proficiency position him as a top-tier offensive line prospect entering the 2026 NFL draft.
With his blend of size, strength, and agility, World's potential to be an effective starter and protect a franchise quarterback is evident. Excelling in pass protection and showcasing power in the ground game, World's ability to neutralize edge rush...

prospects.name: Harold Perkins
prospects.scoutingReport:
Harold Perkins Jr. embodies the modern linebacker prototype, emphasizing speed and versatility over traditional size and power. His rare athleticism, evident in his ability to chase down running backs and close on receivers effortlessly, makes him an enticing prospect for defensive coordinators seeking a dynamic playmaker. While concerns about his size linger, recent NFL success stories like the Ravens, 49ers, and Dolphins have proven the impact undersized but athletic linebackers can have in today's game. Perkins' projection as a WILL linebacker or STAR defender highlights his potential to excel in hybrid defenses that prioritize position flexibility and matchup advantages.

The ACL injury that sidelined Perkins last season adds an element of uncertainty to his draft stock, but his decision to return to LSU provides an opportunity to showcase his full recovery and refine technical aspects of his game. Improving his functional strength and pass-rushing repertoire could elevate Perkins to Pro Bowl caliber. Even without significant physical development, his blend of speed, instincts, and versatility positions him as a game-changing force on defense. Teams eyeing a late first-round or early second-round selection should consider Perkins as a dynamic asset capable of energizing their defensive unit.
From his explosive first step to his elite closing speed, Perkins excels as a pass rusher off the edge, consistently disrupting offensive game plans with his exceptional burst and agility. His alignment versatility allows him to thrive in various roles, from blitzing A-gap defender to coverage linebacker, showcasing his adaptability and potential impact as a chess piece in defensive schemes. While his size limitations present challenges in run defense, Perkins' range, instincts, and relentless pursuit of the ball carrier compensate for these shortcomings, making him a valuable asset in pursuit situations and as a coverage linebacker.

prospects.name: Taylen Green
prospects.scoutingReport:
Standing at 6’6 and weighing in at 235 pounds, Taylen Green is an intriguing prospect with an NFL-caliber arm and impressive mobility. His ability to make throws into tight windows and deliver passes from one hash to the opposite sideline demonstrates his arm strength, a key trait that scouts look for in potential NFL quarterbacks. Despite his promising physical attributes, Green's consistency remains a significant question mark as he has shown a tendency to make questionable throws and lacks the mental traits of a seasoned NFL QB. With a completion rate of 64% and 12 passing touchdowns in four games against tough opponents like Ole Miss and Memphis, Green has shown flashes of his potential but also has room for improvement, particularly in terms of accuracy.

Boasting impressive numbers that have caught the attention of NFL scouts, including leading the FBS in total yards, touchdowns responsible for, and passing touchdowns, Green's performance on the field has been nothing short of impressive. Even in losses, such as the recent road game against Ole Miss, Green has displayed flashes of accuracy on the move that showcase his potential as a dual-threat quarterback. While he may still be considered raw as a passer, Green's undeniable traits have garnered interest from teams looking for a dynamic playmaker at the quarterback position.
Having made the transition from Boise State to Arkansas, Green has already made an impact by helping the Razorbacks secure victories over Texas Tech and Tennessee. His athleticism, arm strength, and NFL-ready size make him a prospect worth watching as he continues to develop as a passer. With continued improvement in his accuracy, Green has the potential to attract even more attention from NFL teams in search of a promising quarterback prospect. While he may need additional time to refine his skills and grow as a player, Green's talent and potential are undeniable, making him a player to watch as he continues his football journey.

His intelligence and leadership skills shine through as he communicates with teammates, aligning them correctly and making adjustments as needed. York's coverage skills are a work in progress, but his ability to blitz, read plays, and anticipate the ball's movement have scouts excited about his potential at the next level. Despite his physical limitations in terms of size and length, his instincts and football IQ compensate for any shortcomings, suggesting a bright future in the NFL. While he may need to work on his consistency as a run defender and finishing tackles, York's speed and agility make him a versatile asset on defense. His dedication to studying film and improving his game sets him apart as one of the smartest linebackers in college football, with play recognition that is second to none. As he continues to develop and refine his skills, there is no doubt that Taurean York will be a force to be reckoned with in the NFL, a player who coaches will rely on to anchor their defense for years to come.

prospects.scoutingReport:
Kaytron Allen, a fourth-year senior running back for Penn State, has been steadily making a name for himself with his strong senior season performance. Despite not having a standout trait and lacking the open-field elusiveness of his peers, Allen's footwork and contact balance at 219 pounds have made him a reliable presence in the backfield. His ability to grind out tough yards and deliver as a decisive downhill hammer with vision and power between the tackles has caught the eye of many. Allen's experience in operating effectively in a timeshare has prepared him for the NFL's modern backfields, where his mentality of moving the chains and softening defensive fronts will be valued. He excels as a one-cut-and-get-downhill runner in both zone and gap schemes, quickly identifying holes and attacking upfield with urgency and little wasted motion, showcasing good contact balance and the ability to consistently fall forward for extra yardage. While Allen may lack home-run speed and quickness in space, his physical running style, toughness, and solid balance after first contact make him an effective goal-line and short-yardage option. His limitations in athleticism and receiving skills may restrict his ceiling initially, but his proven production and rock-solid floor make him a safe bet to contribute with 15+ carries weekly in the NFL. Allen's demeanor as a ball carrier will shine in the right system, where he can complement a flashier featured back and make an impact with his decisive running style and strong lower half. Ultimately, Allen's consistency, physicality, and readiness to contribute from day one position him as a valuable asset for any NFL team looking to add a reliable and hard-nosed back to their roster.

prospects.name: Aiden Fisher
prospects.scoutingReport:
Indiana linebacker Aiden Fisher emerged as a standout performer in a recent game against Oregon, showcasing his versatility as a blitzer, run defender, and coverage player. Despite not possessing ideal size, Fisher compensates with exceptional play speed and a relentless competitive spirit that NFL teams value. Fisher, a transfer from James Madison, has hit his stride as a senior, solidifying his status as a draft-worthy prospect. His impact on the field, particularly in limiting the potent Oregon offense to just 13 points, reflects his leadership qualities and football intelligence that have caught the attention of NFL scouts. Additionally, Fisher's contribution on special teams further illustrates his value as a well-rounded player.

Having earned recognition as Indiana's first-ever All-American linebacker, Fisher's impressive stat line from the previous season includes 118 tackles and 5.5 tackles for loss. Despite his production, Fisher's physical limitations, such as struggles in coverage leading to a high completion rate and an average speed, may hinder his draft positioning to potentially a day-three selection in the upcoming 2026 NFL Draft. While his measurable metrics may not match up with other linebackers from Power-4 conferences, Fisher's football acumen, toughness, and consistent performance suggest he could carve out a role as a reliable NFL backup.
Fisher's draft outlook hinges heavily on how his athleticism translates to the professional level. Although his on-field production in the Big Ten is impressive, the lack of elite measurables raises questions about his potential testing numbers in pre-draft evaluations. Nevertheless, any team that takes a chance on Fisher can expect a natural leader who brings a strong work ethic and a team-first mentality. Fisher's journey from James Madison to Indiana has been a testament to his growth as a player and his ability to excel at the highest level of college football. prospects.name: Demond Claiborne
prospects.scoutingReport:
In 2024, Claiborne demonstrated his prowess as a running back with three 100-yard games against ACC opponents, with his first notable performance nearly leading Wake Forest to an upset win against Georgia Tech. Despite being undersized at 5’9” and 200 lbs., Claiborne's agility and twitchiness make him a formidable opponent for defenders, utilizing his loose athleticism, foot quickness, and sharp cuts to evade tackles and exploit gaps. His ability to string together multiple cuts and access cutback lanes showcases his versatility on the field. Claiborne's explosive running style was evident in his impressive touchdown runs against Georgia Tech, where he showcased his acceleration and elusive speed to evade defenders and reach the end zone.

While Claiborne's performance against Georgia Tech was marred by a fumble, his overall track record of only one fumble in over 450 career touches speaks to his ball security. However, his limitations as a three-down running back in the NFL due to his lack of contributions as a pass catcher and pass protector may relegate him to a Day 3 change-of-pace role. Claiborne's violent cuts and darting moves make him a challenge for defenders to contain, but he must be cautious of unnecessary cuts that could lead to inefficiencies in his running game.
Despite his small stature, Claiborne's elite technical skills, efficient footwork, and high-level vision make him a dynamic playmaker on the field. He excels at making the right decisions and capitalizing on opportunities with his excellent acceleration, translating into big plays and versatility as a receiver out of the backfield. While his size may be a concern for some teams, Claiborne's potential as a part-time playmaker and possibly on special teams could make him an attractive prospect in the upcoming NFL Draft.

prospects.name: Cade Klubnik
prospects.scoutingReport:
Cade Klubnik, a talented quarterback with a dual-threat skill set fitting for today's NFL, has shown significant growth in various areas. His ability to diagnose and read defenses pre-snap has improved, allowing him to attack weaknesses identified before the snap. Klubnik has a good arm for making high-end throws from the pocket and on the move, delivering passes with velocity and accuracy in the short and intermediate parts of the field. His athleticism enables him to make plays with his legs and turn broken plays into positive outcomes for the offense.

While Klubnik's accuracy is improving, it remains a work in progress, particularly under defensive pressure. His decision-making can be questionable when not utilizing his athleticism, leading to potential turnovers. Klubnik is still learning to trust his eyes and progressions, sometimes struggling to pivot to wide-open receivers down the field. His next step in development involves handling post-snap defensive rotations and changes, an area where he currently struggles to process new information quickly. Despite these challenges, Klubnik's talent and potential as a dual-threat quarterback with franchise QB ceiling are evident.
Klubnik's profile as a modern NFL quarterback with dual-threat capabilities and improving pocket presence appeals to offensive coordinators looking for versatility. His arm talent, mobility, and growth curve suggest a high-ceiling prospect who could thrive in systems emphasizing his strengths. The ideal fit for Klubnik would be with a team running a spread-based offense incorporating quarterback runs and moving pockets, where his deep ball accuracy and pre-snap recognition could shine. His decision to return to school for the 2025 season provides an opportunity for further development, especially in post-snap processing and functional strength necessary for the NFL game.

prospects.name: Mikail Kamara
prospects.scoutingReport:
Mikail Kamara, the undersized but technically polished rusher from Indiana, has been making waves in the college football world with his efficient and disruptive play. Standing at 6-foot-1, Kamara may not have the ideal size for an NFL edge rusher, but his skills, instincts, and leadership qualities make him an intriguing prospect for many teams. Kamara excels at winning battles with his hands, leverage, and instincts, allowing him to consistently disrupt opposing offenses and be a productive force on the field. Despite not being the most explosive athlete, Kamara's ability to thrive in his role as a technician sets him apart from other edge rushers in the 2026 NFL Draft class.

His production in the Big Ten has been impressive, with 47 tackles, 15 tackles for loss, and 10 sacks in a single season. However, questions about his NFL fit have arisen due to his lack of length and size compared to traditional edge rushers. To address these concerns, Indiana has moved Kamara to the STUD position, a hybrid EDGE/outside linebacker role, to showcase his versatility and expand his skill set. This shift should provide Kamara with more opportunities for one-on-one rushes and coverage responsibilities, allowing him to further demonstrate his abilities to potential NFL suitors.
While Kamara may not fit the mold of a prototypical NFL edge rusher, his high floor, NFL-ready skill set, and continued development in new roles make him an enticing prospect for teams looking to add a disruptive force to their defense. With the right coaching and support, Kamara has the potential to solidify himself as a valuable asset in the 2026 NFL Draft and beyond, proving that size isn't everything when it comes to making an impact on the field.

prospects.name: Xavier Nwankpa
prospects.scoutingReport:
Looking ahead to his NFL prospects, Xavier Nwankpa presents himself as a versatile safety with the potential to make an immediate impact in various defensive schemes. His rare combination of size, speed, and instincts makes him an appealing option for teams in search of a playmaker in the secondary. Nwankpa's ability to excel both in deep coverage and in the box gives defensive coordinators the flexibility to utilize him in multiple roles, creating mismatches and confusion for opposing offenses. While he may require some time to adjust to the speed and complexity of NFL offenses, Nwankpa's football IQ and work ethic suggest a quick learning curve, especially with his experience in Iowa's pro-style defense.

His film analysis reveals a player who has shown glimpses of elite safety skills, particularly in his run support and physicality. His ability to track the football and attack downhill with explosive speed showcases his potential to be a difference-maker on the field. Nwankpa's versatility in lining up at various defensive positions and his athletic ability under Phil Parker's defensive scheme highlight his adaptability and potential to unlock other players on the defense. However, his coverage instincts are still developing, and he can struggle at times with reading route combinations and anticipating receiver movements, particularly in the red zone.
Strengths in Nwankpa's game include his excellent size, length, explosiveness, and range, making him a solid fit as a box safety with room to add weight. His secure tackling technique and willingness to engage with offensive linemen demonstrate his physical playstyle and functional strength, especially near the line of scrimmage. Despite his limitations in coverage and reaction speed, Nwankpa's athletic ability and potential for growth position him as a mid-round pick with the capacity to contribute effectively, particularly in run support roles.

prospects.name: Roman Hemby
prospects.scoutingReport:
Roman Hemby, a running back with an impressive track record in college football, has shown promise despite facing challenges in recent seasons. With a total of 2,347 rushing yards over four years at Maryland, Hemby has demonstrated his ability to make significant contributions on the ground. However, due to the nature of Maryland's pass-heavy offense, his rushing opportunities were limited, but a move to Indiana offers him a chance to showcase his skills in a more balanced environment. The upcoming 2025 season is crucial for Hemby, as it represents his final opportunity to prove his readiness for the NFL draft.

Hemby's athleticism and top-end speed make him stand out as a potential NFL prospect, but his declining production over the last two seasons raises concerns. The 2025 campaign at Indiana presents him with the perfect platform to address these doubts, especially with the expectation that he will take on a prominent role as the team's primary running back. Hemby's versatility as a pass-catcher further enhances his value, as evidenced by his impressive career receptions tally of 112 for 921 yards and five touchdowns. His ability to contribute in multiple facets of the game makes him a valuable asset to any team.
In addition to his prowess as a receiver, Hemby possesses a unique blend of toughness and speed that sets him apart on the field. His background as a track athlete allows him to accelerate quickly and break away for long gains, while his determination to fight for extra yards through contact showcases his resilience. Despite his strengths, Hemby faces limitations in terms of changing direction and eluding defenders, which impacts his effectiveness as a receiver. However, if he can surpass the 1,000-yard rushing mark in the upcoming season, his draft stock could see a significant rise.
